name: African Studies
elective_note: 9 additional credits from approved African Studies courses (at least 9 cr at 2000-level+, max 6 cr from any single discipline)

name: Anthropology
elective_note: 9 more credits: 1 open-level ANTH elective + 2 intermediate/advanced ANTH courses (2000-level+)

name: Art
elective_note: 12 additional credits: 3 courses from ARTS 2100-3990 (intermediate/advanced) + 1 open-level ARTH or ARTS course. Requires departmental admission after foundations.

name: Art History
elective_note: 12 additional credits: 3-4 courses from ARTH 2000-2990, ARTH 3990, or ARTH 4990

name: Asian Studies
elective_note: 10+ credits from at least 2 disciplines: ANTH 2170, HST 2443/2447/2448, PHIL 2760, POLS 2715, REL 2220/2235/2310, WLIT 2310-2375, plus open-level options

name: Biochemistry
elective_note: Requires Organic Chemistry sequence (CHEM 2580+2585 or CHEM 1500+1550). Prerequisites typically include General Chemistry (CHEM 1400+1450).

name: Biology
elective_note: 9-12 credits: 1 lecture BIOL elective (2100-3699), 1 lab BIOL course (4100-4699), plus 2 additional BCOR/BIOL courses

name: Business Administration
elective_note: 3 additional BUS courses at 2000-level or above (9 credits). Prerequisites: ECON 1400 and ECON 1450 (not counted toward minor credits).

name: Canadian Studies
elective_note: 6 more credits from Canadian History courses, GEOG 2715, or FREN 4680. Prereq: French through FREN 1200.

name: Chemistry
elective_note: Track 1 (Organic): CHEM 2580+2585 + 1 elective (CHEM 2310/2400/2600). Track 2 (Physical): CHEM 3602+3610+3620 + 1 organic course. 19 credits total.

name: Chinese
elective_note: Prereq: Chinese through CHIN 1200. 15 credits total.

name: Civic Engagement
elective_note: 9 credits from approved thematic categories (Contemporary Challenges, Ethical Deliberation, Civil Discourse, Theory/Policy/Governance, Social Change, Engaged Art/Science) + 3 credits Civic/Service Learning (CL/SL) course

name: Classics
elective_note: Track 1 (Classical Studies): 1 language course (GRK or LAT) + 5 CLAS/ARTH 2100 courses, min 12 cr at 2000+. Track 2 (Classical Languages): 9-15 cr of GRK/LAT at 2000+ + 1 CLAS course.

name: Computer Science
elective_note: 12 additional credits in CS courses at the 2000-level or above

name: Critical Race and Ethnic Studies
elective_note: 9 credits: 2-3 courses at 2000-level+ (6-9 cr) + up to 1 open-level course (0-3 cr)

name: Dance
elective_note: 9 more credits: 6 cr from DNCE 2000+ + 3 cr from any DNCE level

name: Economics
elective_note: 12 additional credits in ECON courses at various levels

name: English
elective_note: 12 more credits: 6-9 cr from ENGL 2060-3880 or FTS courses (intermediate+) + 1 open-level ENGL elective

name: Environmental Studies
elective_note: 9 credits across Environmental Humanities (ENVS 2200-2299), Natural Science (ENVS 2600-2699), and Social Science (ENVS 2400-2499) categories

name: European Studies
elective_note: 12 more credits: European Culture & Thought (ARTH, CLAS, ENGL, MU, PHIL, POLS) + European History & Society (CLAS, HST) + 1 advanced course (3000+ in English or 4000+ in language)

name: Film and Television Studies
elective_note: 9 more credits: 2 open-level FTS courses (6 cr) + 1 intermediate/advanced FTS course (3 cr)

name: French
elective_note: 6-9 cr from FREN 3100-4700 (intermediate) + 3 cr from FREN 4100-4700 (advanced). Prereq: FREN 1200 or equivalent.

name: Gender, Sexuality, and Women's Studies
elective_note: 9 more credits: 6-9 cr from GSWS 2600-3990 or cross-listed courses (intermediate+) + up to 3 cr open-level

name: Geography
elective_note: 6 cr from GEOG 2000-4990 or GEOL 3410 (intermediate/advanced) + 3 cr open-level GEOG

name: Geosciences
elective_note: 6-8 cr from Earth Materials, Land-Surface Processes, and Atmospheric/Climatological topics. 15 credits total.

name: Geospatial Technologies
elective_note: 3 more credits from GIS, Remote Sensing, or Data Science categories. At least 9 cr at 2000-level+.

name: German
elective_note: 18 credits total: 15 cr from GERM 2100-4995 + 1 course from GERM 4500-4990. Prereq: GERM 1200.

name: Global Studies
elective_note: 9 cr from one concentration at 2000-level+: Political-Economic, Human/Environmental, Humanities, Asian Studies, European Studies, or Latin American/Caribbean

name: Health and Society
elective_note: 6 more credits: 1-2 additional courses at 2000-level+ from approved interdisciplinary options. 21 credits total.

name: History

choose_one_groups.description: Geographic area 2 (choose 1 HST from different region)
Geographic area 3 (choose 1 HST from different region)
Geographic area 4 (choose 1 HST from different region)

elective_note: 6 additional credits in HST courses at any level. Must span at least 2 of 4 geographic regions.

name: Holocaust Studies
elective_note: 12 cr: 1 intermediate HST/SOC course at 2000-level+ + 3 open-level electives from WLIT 1150/1155 or related courses

name: Italian Studies
elective_note: 18 credits: 6+ cr from ITAL 3100-3990 + supporting courses from Classics, History, Religion, Theatre, WLIT, or ARTH. Prereq: ITAL 1200. Max 6 cr from any single non-Italian prefix.

name: Japanese

options.subject: WLIT
options.title: Topics in Japanese Lit in Translation

elective_note: 15 credits total. Prereq: JAPN 1200.

name: Jewish Studies
elective_note: 12 more credits: 1-2 intermediate courses (6-9 cr) + 2 open-level courses (6 cr) from JS 1000+ offerings

name: Latin American and Caribbean Studies
elective_note: 12 credits from at least 2 disciplines: CDAE 1030, DNCE 1430, ENGL 2244, HST 1472/1475/2472/2545, MU 1175, POLS 2720/3730, SPAN 3665/3670, WLIT 1022. At least 9 cr at 2000+ (or 3000+ for language).

name: Law and Society
elective_note: 12-15 cr: 4-5 courses from POLS 2315-2379/4310, SOC 2230-3259, and approved interdisciplinary courses (BUS, ECON, PHIL, PSYS, REL, etc.). At least 9 cr at 2000-level+, max 9 cr from any single prefix.

name: Linguistics
elective_note: 9 more credits: 6 cr from any LING 1010-4995 + 3 cr from LING 2200-4995

name: Mathematics
elective_note: 9 additional credits in MATH 2000-level or above (excluding MATH 2111 and MATH 2180)

name: Middle East Studies
elective_note: 9 more credits from HST 4420, REL 2235, POLS 2525/JS 1610/REL 1230 (if not used above), plus Arabic/Hebrew language (up to 6 cr). At least 9 cr at 2000-level+.

name: Music
elective_note: 9 more credits: 3 cr additional theory/composition (MU 1310/1770/2770/2300-3399) + 6 cr performance (private lessons/ensembles). At least 9 cr at 2000-level+.

name: Music Technology and Business
elective_note: 3-6 more credits from MU 1775, 2770, 2771, 2772, 3771, 4770, 4771. At least 9 cr at 2000-level+.

name: Musical Theatre
elective_note: 2 cr from private voice lessons or performing ensemble (MUE 2125, MUE 2300, or MUL 2400). Cannot major in Music or Theatre.

name: Neuroscience
elective_note: 12 credits across Behavioral/Cognitive (PSYS 2100-3299, CSD courses) and Cellular/Molecular (BCOR 2300, NSCI 3000+, PHRM 3010) categories. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400, general chemistry, two biology courses.

name: Philosophy
elective_note: 18 credits total: 1 History of Philosophy course + 3 open-level + 1 intermediate + 1 advanced

name: Political Science
elective_note: 12 additional credits in POLS courses (2200-level or above recommended)

name: Psychological Science
elective_note: One advanced PSYS course (3100–3990 or 4990)

name: Public Policy Analysis
elective_note: 3-6 cr from POLS policy electives (2315-3490) + 0-3 cr from ECON policy electives (2110-2800). 21 credits total.

name: Religion
elective_note: 6 more credits open-level from REL courses, CAS 1710, or related religious studies offerings

name: Reporting and Documentary Storytelling
elective_note: 18 credits total: writing + media theory + 3 practice courses + internship

name: Russian
elective_note: 12 more credits from RUSS 2100-4996, or WLIT 1250/2250 (up to 3 cr). Prereq: RUSS 1200. 20 credits total.

name: Russian/East European Studies
elective_note: 9 more credits from HST 2698/2750/2790, POLS 2710/2712/3610, WLIT 1250/2250. At least 9 cr at 2000-level+. Prereq: RUSS 1200.

name: Sexuality and Gender Identity Studies
elective_note: 9 more credits: 6-9 cr from GSWS 2600-3660 or cross-listed intermediate courses + up to 3 cr open-level

name: Sociology
elective_note: 9 additional credits in SOC courses (open, intermediate, and advanced levels)

name: Spanish
elective_note: 6 cr from SPAN 4100-4990 (upper-level electives). Prereq: SPAN 2100 or equivalent.

name: Statistics
elective_note: 6 additional credits in STAT courses

name: Theatre
elective_note: 18 credits total. Cannot major in Theatre.

name: Writing
elective_note: 18 credits: 2 intro writing + 2 intermediate writing + 1 writing & literature + 1 elective

name: Zoology
elective_note: 9-12 cr: 2 lecture BIOL courses (2100-3699) + 1 lab BIOL course (4100-4699) + 1 additional BCOR/BIOL course. Focus on animal biology.
